generatedSentence: Uncle Silas, overtly wearing his waistcoat, tried to cajole Elara upstairs in the ancient castle, hoping to underwhelm her understanding of the verified inheritance. He'd nursed his phlegm near the oven, dreaming of global rockets and jackets, while she felt the scar of his attempts to oppress, a curious pickle. A ground trap, protecting secret caches of coconuts on a mountain, awaited her. Elara, intrigued by a mythical worm's cure, knew this quest was important; everything hinged on her. Her birthday election as an elector had brought her into a duel with the mafia's clever trickster, who was constantly scamming. A crimson painting, a voxel rendering, detailed the worm's doom and promised nirvana to the victorious, a true knight's glory. Applying ivory utensils, she knew this wasn't normal; karma was at play. The trickster's plan, a global transporter, was a trap of tangled electrics and mirrors, aiming to tempt Elara into a foolish tenancy. He hoped to underwhelm her. During a sudden blackout, a gunshot rang out, and a jaguar, an acrobat of sorts, lunged. She saw his true condition: a musket-wielding butcher, hiding his face behind hijabs, ready to find and capture her mind, reducing her to unemployment. Elara, finding joysticks and armed with an eighties rocket, lightened her burden, ready to vibrate and fight. Wounded, the trickster began to jabber, rejected, his aura of acclaim fading as typhoons of regret swirled around him. She watched monkeys and a moose run, the prison of his mind finally shattering. She had found her cure, achieving nirvana, leaving behind the karmic wormhole.
panelSentences: Uncle Silas, overtly wearing his waistcoat, tried to cajole Elara upstairs in the ancient castle, hoping to underwhelm her understanding of the verified inheritance. He'd nursed his phlegm near the oven, dreaming of global rockets and jackets, while she felt the scar of his attempts to oppress, a curious pickle. A ground trap, protecting secret caches of coconuts on a mountain, awaited her.

Elara, intrigued by a mythical worm's cure, knew this quest was important; everything hinged on her. Her birthday election as an elector had brought her into a duel with the mafia's clever trickster, who was constantly scamming. A crimson painting, a voxel rendering, detailed the worm's doom and promised nirvana to the victorious, a true knight's glory. Applying ivory utensils, she knew this wasn't normal; karma was at play.

The trickster's plan, a global transporter, was a trap of tangled electrics and mirrors, aiming to tempt Elara into a foolish tenancy. He hoped to underwhelm her. During a sudden blackout, a gunshot rang out, and a jaguar, an acrobat of sorts, lunged. She saw his true condition: a musket-wielding butcher, hiding his face behind hijabs, ready to find and capture her mind, reducing her to unemployment.

Elara, finding joysticks and armed with an eighties rocket, lightened her burden, ready to vibrate and fight. Wounded, the trickster began to jabber, rejected, his aura of acclaim fading as typhoons of regret swirled around him. She watched monkeys and a moose run, the prison of his mind finally shattering. She had found her cure, achieving nirvana, leaving behind the karmic wormhole.
rawPanelSentences: Uncle Silas, overtly wearing his waistcoat, tried to cajole Elara upstairs in the ancient castle, hoping to underwhelm her understanding of the verified inheritance. He'd nursed his phlegm near the oven, dreaming of global rockets and jackets, while she felt the scar of his attempts to oppress, a curious pickle. A ground trap, protecting secret caches of coconuts on a mountain, awaited her.

Elara, intrigued by a mythical worm's cure, knew this quest was important; everything hinged on her. Her birthday election as an elector had brought her into a duel with the mafia's clever trickster, who was constantly scamming. A crimson painting, a voxel rendering, detailed the worm's doom and promised nirvana to the victorious, a true knight's glory. Applying ivory utensils, she knew this wasn't normal; karma was at play.

The trickster's plan, a global transporter, was a trap of tangled electrics and mirrors, aiming to tempt Elara into a foolish tenancy. He hoped to underwhelm her. During a sudden blackout, a gunshot rang out, and a jaguar, an acrobat of sorts, lunged. She saw his true condition: a musket-wielding butcher, hiding his face behind hijabs, ready to find and capture her mind, reducing her to unemployment.

Elara, finding joysticks and armed with an eighties rocket, lightened her burden, ready to vibrate and fight. Wounded, the trickster began to jabber, rejected, his aura of acclaim fading as typhoons of regret swirled around him. She watched monkeys and a moose run, the prison of his mind finally shattering. She had found her cure, achieving nirvana, leaving behind the karmic wormhole.
imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, vibrant neon futurism, bold ink outlines, cinematic lighting, thick white gutters, clean black borders. Protagonist: A determined young woman with bright crimson hair and a metallic, segmented arm. TOP-LEFT PANEL: Young Elara, in a futuristic waistcoat, stands defiantly inside a crumbling neo-gothic castle, looking towards a holographic mountain vista. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: Elara duels a shadowy, cloaked figure wielding an energy whip, reflected in a distorted mirror, surrounded by floating voxel art. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: Elara, amidst a sudden blackout, dodges a leaping cyber-jaguar in a tight, glowing-wire-filled room, a single gunshot flash illuminating the scene. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: Elara, victorious, holds a glowing joystick-like device, overlooking a serene landscape with a shimmering wormhole receding in the distance.

definitions.definition: A user of the Twitter microblogging service whose identity has been confirmed by Twitter.
Subject to positive verification.
definitions.example: Only verified cardholders are eligible for the discount.

definitions.definition: To substantiate or prove the truth of something
To confirm or test the truth or accuracy of something
To affirm something formally, under oath
In an overt manner; publicly; openly.
A chamber used for baking or heating.
To fail to impress; to perform disappointingly.
A large building that is fortified and contains many defences; in previous ages often inhabited by a nobleman or king.
An instance of castling.
A rook; a chess piece shaped like a castle tower.
A defense structure in shogi formed by defensive pieces surrounding the king.
A close helmet.
Any strong, imposing, and stately mansion.
A small tower, as on a ship, or an elephant's back.
The wicket.
To house or keep in a castle.
To protect or separate in a similar way.
To make into a castle: to build in the form of a castle or add (real or imitation) battlements to an existing building.

(usually intransitive) To move the king 2 squares right or left and, in the same turn, the nearest rook to the far side of the king. The move now has special rules: the king cannot be in, go through, or end in check; the squares between the king and rook must be vacant; and neither piece may have been moved before castling.
(usually intransitive) To create a similar defensive position in Japanese chess through several moves.
To bowl a batsman with a full-length ball or yorker such that the stumps are knocked over.
Funding by many individuals pooling their money together for a common goal, usually via the Internet.
To persuade someone to do something which they are reluctant to do, especially by flattery or promises; to coax.
A fruit of the coconut palm (not a true nut), Cocos nucifera, having a fibrous husk surrounding a large seed.
A hard-shelled seed of this fruit, having white flesh and a fluid-filled central cavity.
The edible white flesh of this fruit.
The coconut palm.
A Hispanic or dark-skinned person who acts “white” (Caucasian), alluding to the fact that a coconut is brown on the outside and white on the inside.
A black person who thinks or acts "white" (European).
A Pacific islander.
A female breast.
The human head (often used in cricket broadcasts when a ball hits or nearly hits a batsman on the head).
A machine or other device designed to catch (and sometimes kill) animals, either by holding them in a container, or by catching hold of part of the body.
definitions.example: I put down some traps in my apartment to try and deal with the mouse problem.

definitions.definition: A trick or arrangement designed to catch someone in a more general sense; a snare.
definitions.example: Unfortunately she fell into the trap of confusing biology with destiny.

definitions.definition: A covering over a hole or opening; a trapdoor.
definitions.example: Close the trap, would you, before someone falls and breaks their neck.

definitions.definition: A kind of movable stepladder or set of stairs.
A wooden instrument shaped somewhat like a shoe, used in the game of trapball
The game of trapball itself.
Any device used to hold and suddenly release an object.
definitions.example: They shot out of the school gates like greyhounds out of the trap.

definitions.definition: A bend, sag, or other device in a waste-pipe arranged so that the liquid contents form a seal which prevents the escape of noxious gases, but permits the flow of liquids.
A place in a water pipe, pump, etc., where air accumulates for lack of an outlet.
A light two-wheeled carriage with springs.
A person's mouth.
definitions.example: Keep your trap shut.

definitions.definition: (in the plural) Belongings.
A cubicle (in a public toilet).
definitions.example: I've just laid a cable in trap 2 so I'd give it 5 minutes if I were you.

definitions.definition: Trapshooting.
A geological structure that creates a petroleum reservoir.
An exception generated by the processor or by an external event.
A mining license inspector during the Australian gold rush.

A vehicle, residential building, or sidewalk corner where drugs are manufactured, packaged, or sold. (Also used attributively to describe things which are used for the sale of drugs, e.g. "a trap phone", "a trap car".)
A fictional character from anime, or related media, who is coded as or has qualities typically associated with a gender other than the character's ostensible gender; otokonoko.
A trans woman, transfeminine person, or crossdressing man.
A genre of hip-hop music, with half-time drums and heavy sub-bass.
The money earned by a prostitute for a pimp.
To physically capture, to catch in a trap or traps, or something like a trap.
definitions.example: to trap foxes

definitions.definition: To ensnare; to take by stratagem; to entrap.
To provide with a trap.
definitions.example: to trap a drain

definitions.definition: To set traps for game; to make a business of trapping game
definitions.example: trap for beaver

definitions.definition: To leave suddenly, to flee.
To sell illegal drugs, especially in a public area.
To capture (e.g. an error) in order to handle or process it.
To attend to and open and close a (trap-)door.
An elevation of land of considerable dimensions rising more or less abruptly, forming a conspicuous figure in the landscape, usually having a small extent of surface at its summit.
definitions.example: We spent the weekend hiking in the mountains.

definitions.definition: Something very large in size or quantity; a huge amount; a great heap.
definitions.example: He was a real mountain of a man, standing seven feet tall.

definitions.definition: Of, belonging to, relating to, or found on a mountain; like a mountain in size; (of living things) growing or living on a mountain.
definitions.example: some mountain ranges; the high mountain top; a scenic mountain view; the mountain lodge; the leaves of mountain cranberries; a mountain goat

definitions.definition: A difficult task or challenge.
A woman's large breast.
The twenty-first Lenormand card.
Wine made from grapes that grow on a mountain.
A store of things that may be required in the future, which can be retrieved rapidly, protected or hidden in some way.
definitions.example: Members of the 29-man Discovery team laid down food caches to allow the polar team to travel light, hopping from food cache to food cache on their return journey.

definitions.definition: A fast temporary storage where recently or frequently used information is stored to avoid having to reload it from a slower storage medium.
(geocaching) A container containing treasure in a global treasure-hunt game.
To breastfeed: to feed (a baby) at the breast; to suckle.
definitions.example: She believes that nursing her baby will make him strong and healthy.

definitions.definition: To breastfeed: to be fed at the breast.
To care for (someone), especially in sickness; to tend to.
definitions.example: She nursed him back to health.

definitions.definition: To treat kindly and with extra care
definitions.example: She nursed the rosebush and that season it bloomed.

definitions.definition: To manage with care and economy
To drink slowly
To foster, to nourish
To hold closely to one's chest
definitions.example: Would you like to nurse the puppy?

definitions.definition: To strike (billiard balls) gently, so as to keep them in good position during a series of shots.
One of the four humors making up the body in ancient and mediaeval medicine; said to be cold and moist, and often identified with mucus.
Viscid mucus produced by the body, later especially mucus expelled from the bronchial passages by coughing.
A watery distillation, especially one obtained from plant matter; an aqueous solution.
Calmness of temperament, composure; also seen negatively, sluggishness, indifference.
A violent flow, as of water, lava, etc.; a stream suddenly raised and running rapidly, as down a precipice.
definitions.example: A torrent of green and white water broke over the hull of the sail-boat.

definitions.definition: A large amount or stream of something.
definitions.example: They endured a torrent of inquiries.

definitions.definition: (file sharing) A set of files obtainable through a peer-to-peer network, especially BitTorrent.
definitions.example: I got a torrent of the complete works of Shakespeare the other day; I'm not sure why.

definitions.definition: To keep down by unjust force.
definitions.example: The rural poor were oppressed by the land-owners.

definitions.definition: To make sad or gloomy.
definitions.example: We were oppressed by the constant grey skies.

definitions.definition: Physically to press down on (someone) with harmful effects; to smother, crush.
Any activity that uses physical exertion or skills competitively under a set of rules that is not based on aesthetics.
Something done for fun, regardless of its design or intended purpose.

definitions.definition: A person who exhibits either good or bad sportsmanship.
definitions.example: Jen may have won, but she was sure a poor sport; she laughed at the loser.

definitions.definition: Somebody who behaves or reacts in an admirably good-natured manner, e.g. to being teased or to losing a game; a good sport.

definitions.definition: That which diverts, and makes mirth; pastime; amusement.
Mockery, making fun; derision.
A toy; a plaything; an object of mockery.
Gaming for money as in racing, hunting, fishing.

A plant or an animal, or part of a plant or animal, which has some peculiarity not usually seen in the species; an abnormal variety or growth. The term encompasses both mutants and organisms with non-genetic developmental abnormalities such as birth defects.
A sportsman; a gambler.
One who consorts with disreputable people, including prostitutes.
An amorous dalliance.
definitions.example: Charlie and Lisa enjoyed a bit of sport after their hike.

definitions.definition: (usually singular) A friend or acquaintance (chiefly used when speaking to the friend in question)
Play; idle jingle.
To amuse oneself, to play.

definitions.definition: To mock or tease, treat lightly, toy with.

definitions.definition: To display; to have as a notable feature.

definitions.definition: To divert; to amuse; to make merry.
To represent by any kind of play.
To practise the diversions of the field or the turf; to be given to betting, as upon races.
To assume suddenly a new and different character from the rest of the plant or from the type of the species; said of a bud, shoot, plant, or animal.

An ornamental garment worn under a doublet.
A sleeveless, collarless garment worn over a shirt and under a suit jacket.

definitions.definition: Up the stairs; on a higher floor or level.

definitions.definition: In heaven, especially with regard to where a deity might be found.
The brother or brother-in-law of one’s parent.
The male cousin of one’s parent.
A companion to one's (usually unmarried) mother.
A source of advice, encouragement, or help.

To act like, or as, an uncle.
A cry used to indicate surrender.
To start (a fire) or light (a torch, a match, coals, etc.).
To arouse or inspire (a passion, etc).

definitions.definition: To begin to grow or take hold.
The surface of the Earth, as opposed to the sky or water or underground.
definitions.example: Look, I found a ten dollar bill on the ground!

definitions.definition: Terrain.
Soil, earth.
definitions.example: The fox escaped from the hounds by going to ground.

definitions.definition: The bottom of a body of water.
Basis, foundation, groundwork, legwork.
(chiefly in the plural) Reason, (epistemic) justification, cause.

definitions.definition: Background, context, framework, surroundings.
The area on which a battle is fought, particularly as referring to the area occupied by one side or the other. Often, according to the eventualities, "to give ground" or "to gain ground".
(by extension) Advantage given or gained in any contest; e.g. in football, chess, debate or academic discourse.
The plain surface upon which the figures of an artistic composition are set.
definitions.example: crimson flowers on a white ground

definitions.definition: In sculpture, a flat surface upon which figures are raised in relief.
In point lace, the net of small meshes upon which the embroidered pattern is applied.

definitions.definition: In etching, a gummy substance spread over the surface of a metal to be etched, to prevent the acid from eating except where an opening is made by the needle.
(chiefly in the plural) One of the pieces of wood, flush with the plastering, to which mouldings etc. are attached.
definitions.example: Grounds are usually put up first and the plastering floated flush with them.

definitions.definition: A soccer stadium.
definitions.example: Manchester United's ground is known as Old Trafford.

definitions.definition: An electrical conductor connected to the earth, or a large conductor whose electrical potential is taken as zero (such as a steel chassis).

The area of grass on which a match is played (a cricket field); the entire arena in which it is played; the part of the field behind a batsman's popping crease where he can not be run out (hence to make one's ground).
A composition in which the bass, consisting of a few bars of independent notes, is continually repeated to a varying melody.
The tune on which descants are raised; the plain song.
The pit of a theatre.
To connect (an electrical conductor or device) to a ground.
To punish, especially a child or teenager, by forcing him/her to stay at home and/or give up certain privileges.
definitions.example: Eric, you are grounded until further notice for lying to us about where you were last night!

definitions.definition: To forbid (an aircraft or pilot) to fly.
definitions.example: Because of the bad weather, all flights were grounded.

definitions.definition: To give a basic education in a particular subject; to instruct in elements or first principles.

definitions.definition: To hit a ground ball. Compare fly (verb(regular)) and line (verb).
To place something on the ground.
To run aground; to strike the bottom and remain fixed.

definitions.definition: To found; to fix or set, as on a foundation, reason, or principle; to furnish a ground for; to fix firmly.
To cover with a ground, as a copper plate for etching, or as paper or other materials with a uniform tint as a preparation for ornament.
To improve or focus the mental or emotional state of.
definitions.example: I ground myself with meditation.

A non-guided missile propelled by a rocket engine.

An angry communication (such as a letter or telegram) to a subordinate.
A blunt lance head used in jousting.
Something that shoots high in the air.
A stupid or crazy person.
To accelerate swiftly and powerfully
To fly vertically
To rise or soar rapidly

To attack something with rockets
A piece of clothing worn on the upper body outside a shirt or blouse, often waist length to thigh length.
A piece of a person's suit, beside trousers and, sometimes, waistcoat; coat (US)
A protective or insulating cover for an object (e.g. a book, hot water tank, bullet.)
A police record.
In ordnance, a strengthening band surrounding and reinforcing the tube in which the charge is fired.
The tough outer skin of a baked potato.
definitions.example: Cook the potatoes in their jackets.

definitions.definition: To enclose or encase in a jacket or other covering.
The passing of title to an estate upon death.
That which a person is entitled to inherit, by law or testament.
The act or mechanism of inheriting; the state of having inherited
definitions.example: The Indo-European languages share various similarities as a result of their inheritance from a common ancestor.

definitions.definition: (genetic algorithms) The biological attributes passed hereditarily from ancestors to their offspring.
The mechanism whereby parts of a superclass are available to instances of its subclass.
A cucumber preserved in a solution, usually a brine or a vinegar syrup.
definitions.example: A pickle goes well with a hamburger.

definitions.definition: (often in the plural) Any vegetable preserved in vinegar and consumed as relish.
The brine used for preserving food.
definitions.example: This tub is filled with the pickle that we will put the small cucumbers into.

definitions.definition: A difficult situation; peril.
definitions.example: The climber found himself in a pickle when one of the rocks broke off.

definitions.definition: A mildly mischievous loved one.
A rundown.
definitions.example: Jones was caught in a pickle between second and third.

definitions.definition: A children’s game with three participants that emulates a baseball rundown
definitions.example: The boys played pickle in the front yard for an hour.

definitions.definition: A penis.
A pipe for smoking methamphetamine.
definitions.example: Load some shards in that pickle.

definitions.definition: A bath of dilute sulphuric or nitric acid, etc., to remove burnt sand, scale, rust, etc., from the surface of castings, or other articles of metal, or to brighten them or improve their colour.
In an optical landing system, the hand-held controller connected to the lens, or apparatus on which the lights are mounted.
To preserve food (or sometimes other things) in a salt, sugar or vinegar solution.
definitions.example: These cucumbers pickle very well.

definitions.definition: To remove high-temperature scale and oxidation from metal with heated (often sulphuric) industrial acid.
definitions.example: The crew will pickle the fittings in the morning.

definitions.definition: (in the Python programming language) To serialize.
To pour brine over a person after flogging them, as a method of punishment.
A permanent mark on the skin, sometimes caused by the healing of a wound.
(by extension) A permanent negative effect on someone's mind, caused by a traumatic experience.
Any permanent mark resulting from damage.
To mark the skin permanently.
To form a scar.
To affect deeply in a traumatic manner.
definitions.example: Seeing his parents die in a car crash scarred him for life.

definitions.definition: Existing in myth.
(by extension) Not real; false or fabricated.
A generally tubular invertebrate of the annelid phylum; an earthworm.
More loosely, any of various tubular invertebrates resembling annelids but not closely related to them, such as velvet worms, acorn worms, flatworms, or roundworms.
A type of wingless "dragon", especially a gigantic sea serpent.
Either a mythical "dragon" (especially wingless), a gigantic sea serpent, or a creature that resembles a Mongolian death worm.
A contemptible or devious being.
definitions.example: Don't try to run away, you little worm!

definitions.definition: A self-replicating program that propagates through a network.
A graphical representation of the total runs scored in an innings.
Anything helical, especially the thread of a screw.
Any creeping or crawling animal, such as a snake, snail, or caterpillar.
An internal tormentor; something that gnaws or afflicts one’s mind with remorse.
A strip of linked tiles sharing parallel edges in a tiling.
The lytta.
(preceded by definite article) A dance, or dance move, in which the dancer lies on the floor and undulates the body horizontally thereby moving forwards.
To make (one's way) with a crawling motion.
definitions.example: We wormed our way through the underbrush.

definitions.definition: To move with one's body dragging the ground.
To work one's way by artful or devious means.
To work (one's way or oneself) (into) gradually or slowly; to insinuate.
definitions.example: He wormed his way into the organization

definitions.definition: To effect, remove, drive, draw, or the like, by slow and secret means; often followed by out.
(in “worm out of”) To drag out of, to get information that someone is reluctant or unwilling to give (through artful or devious means or by pleading or asking repeatedly).
To fill in the contlines of (a rope) before parcelling and serving.
definitions.example: Worm and parcel with the lay; turn and serve the other way.

definitions.definition: To deworm (an animal).
To cut the worm, or lytta, from under the tongue of (a dog, etc.) for the purpose of checking a disposition to gnaw, and formerly supposed to guard against canine madness.
To clean by means of a worm; to draw a wad or cartridge from, as a firearm.
A method, device or medication that restores good health.
Act of healing or state of being healed; restoration to health after a disease, or to soundness after injury.
A solution to a problem.
A process of preservation, as by smoking.
A process of solidification or gelling.
A process whereby a material is caused to form permanent molecular linkages by exposure to chemicals, heat, pressure and/or weathering.
Care, heed, or attention.
Spiritual charge; care of soul; the office of a parish priest or of a curate.
That which is committed to the charge of a parish priest or of a curate.
The sum total of a person's actions, which determine the person's next incarnation in samsara, the cycle of death and rebirth.
A force or law of nature which causes one to reap what one sows; destiny; fate.
A distinctive feeling, aura, or atmosphere.
(Reddit) A score assigned to a user of a discussion forum, indicating the popularity of their posts with other users.
Having relevant and crucial value.
definitions.example: It is very important to give your daughter independence in her life so she learns from experience.

definitions.definition: Pompous; self-important.
Destiny, especially terrible.
An undesirable fate; an impending severe occurrence or danger that seems inevitable.
A feeling of danger, impending danger, darkness or despair.
A law.
A judgment or decision.
A sentence or penalty for illegal behaviour.
Death.
definitions.example: They met an untimely doom when the mineshaft caved in.

definitions.definition: (sometimes capitalized) The Last Judgment; or, an artistic representation thereof.
To pronounce sentence or judgment on; to condemn.
definitions.example: a criminal doomed to death

definitions.definition: To destine; to fix irrevocably the ill fate of.
To judge; to estimate or determine as a judge.
To ordain as a penalty; hence, to mulct or fine.
To assess a tax upon, by estimate or at discretion.
All the things under discussion.
definitions.example: I checked the list again and everything is done.

definitions.definition: Many or most things.
definitions.example:
A:What do you want to do at the amusement park?
B:Everything!

definitions.definition: A state of well-being (from all parts of the whole).
definitions.example: She wasn't feeling well this morning but now everything is fine.

definitions.definition: Considerable effort.
definitions.example: It took everything in me to resist the temptation to skip work on my birthday.

definitions.definition: The most important thing.
definitions.example: I can't believe I made it in time - timing is everything!

definitions.definition: The three-dimensional analogue of a pixel; a volume element representing some numerical quantity, such as the colour, of a point in three-dimensional space, used in the visualisation and analysis of three-dimensional (especially scientific and medical) data
The hard white form of dentin which forms the tusks of elephants, walruses and other animals.
A creamy white color, the color of ivory.
Something made from or resembling ivory.
(collective, singular or in plural) The teeth.
(collective, singular or in plural) The keys of a piano.
A white person.
Made of ivory.
Resembling or having the colour of ivory.
To conceive or carry out a secret plan intended to harm; to form a plot or scheme.
To arouse the interest of; to fascinate.
To have clandestine or illicit intercourse.
To fill with artifice and duplicity; to complicate.
To apply paint to.
To apply in the manner that paint is applied.
To cover (something) with spots of colour, like paint.
To create (an image) with paints.
definitions.example: to paint a portrait or a landscape

definitions.definition: To practise the art of painting pictures.
definitions.example: I've been painting since I was a young child.

definitions.definition: To draw an element in a graphical user interface.
To depict or portray.
definitions.example: She sued the author of the biography, claiming it painted her as a duplicitous fraud.

definitions.definition: To color one's face by way of beautifying it.
To direct a radar beam toward.

Being the winner in a contest, struggle, war, etc.

The wood of the olive tree.
A dark yellowish-green color, that of an unripe olive.
An olivary body, part of the medulla oblongata.

definitions.definition: Any shell of the genus Oliva and allied genera; so called from the shape.
An oystercatcher, a shore bird.
To defraud or embezzle.

definitions.definition: Something that protrudes, sticks out, or sticks together; a cluster or blob; a mound or mass of no particular shape.

definitions.definition: A group, set, or unit.

definitions.definition: A dull or lazy person.

A kind of fish, the lumpsucker.
Food given to a tramp to be eaten on the road.
A beating or verbal abuse.

Having a deep red colour.
Immodest.

Resourceful, sometimes to the point of cunning.

Fit; suitable; having propriety.
Well-shaped; handsome.
Good-natured; obliging.
Fit and healthy; free from fatigue or illness.

Victory; success.
An emanation of light supposed to shine from beings that are specially holy. It is represented in art by rays of gold, or the like, proceeding from the head or body, or by a disk, or a mere line.

Pride; boastfulness; arrogance.
To exult with joy; to rejoice.
To boast; to be proud.
To shine radiantly.

Fencing with the use of a body wire, box, and related equipment to detect when a weapon has touched an opponent.

definitions.definition: The mandatory blocking of all light emanating from buildings as imposed during World War II.
A large aquatic rodent (Ondatra zibethicus).
Any of several species of shrews in the family Soricidae, especially the Asian house shrew, Suncus murinus

definitions.definition: Continual prayer on a dead person's behalf for a period after their death.

definitions.example: Unemployment has been considered a cause of crime.

definitions.definition: The level of joblessness in an economy, often measured as a percentage of the workforce.
definitions.example: Unemployment was reported at 5.2% in May, up from 4.9% in April.

definitions.definition: A type of joblessness due to a particular economic mechanism.
definitions.example: All unemployments, seasonal, frictional, cyclical, classical, whatever, mean that you're out of work.

definitions.definition: An instance or period of joblessness.
definitions.example: Until then his life had consisted of low-paying jobs, numberous unemployments, and drug use.
